description: A strong, regular, repeated pattern of movement or sound. 'Telui' names the pulse beneath—the beat that structures time, the pattern that repeats and carries.
sound_symbolism: The word pulses steadily. 'Te' marks the beat, rhythm starting. 'Lu' flows to the next pulse. Finally 'i' rises to repeat, the pattern continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo telui' (the rhythm). Related to 'hena' (pattern) but emphasizes temporal repetition rather than spatial arrangement.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Rhythm structured ornament—repeating motifs that carried the eye through designs in regular flow.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Life followed rhythms—seasonal, lunar, daily patterns that structured activity and expectation.
